edges.description: Excess active TGF-beta 1 engages non-canonical Rho GTPase signaling that increases osteoclast formation, migration, and resorptive activity.
Overactive TGF-beta signaling disrupts coordinated bone formation and resorption, producing high-turnover cortical sclerosis.
Increased osteoclast formation and resorption contribute to uncoupled high-turnover bone remodeling.
Periosteal and endosteal new bone formation produces progressive diaphyseal hyperostosis and sclerosis.
Endosteal cortical thickening narrows the medullary cavity of long bones.
High-turnover cortical remodeling and narrowed marrow spaces contribute to bone pain.
Long-bone cortical thickening and medullary narrowing are associated with muscular weakness in CED.
Lower-limb cortical thickening, pain, and muscular weakness contribute to a wide-based waddling gait.
The same hyperostotic remodeling can involve the skull and skull base.
Skull-base hyperostosis can narrow foramina and compress cranial nerves.
Skull and temporal-bone involvement can compromise auditory structures and require hearing-loss management.
Cranial hyperostosis in severe CED can produce proptosis.
Severe cranial involvement can cause intracranial-pressure complications.

edges.source: Bone Density, Blood Count, and Disease Activity Monitoring

nodes.description: Pain management with NSAIDs and analgesics for symptomatic relief.

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann Disease.
evidence.snippet: pain is also managed with analgesics, non-pharmacologic methods, and on occasion surgical treatment.

nodes.description: Annual audiology and otolaryngology care monitor and manage hearing loss; myringotomy may help conductive hearing loss from serous otitis.

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann Disease.
evidence.snippet: Management of hearing loss per otolaryngologist; bilateral myringotomy can improve conductive hearing loss resulting from serous otitis.

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann Disease.
evidence.snippet: annual audiology evaluation with BAER and inner ear CT as needed;

nodes.description: Monitor musculoskeletal disease activity, corticosteroid-related bone density risk, blood pressure during targeted therapy, and annual CBC for marrow consequences of medullary-cavity narrowing.
nodes.id: Bone Density, Blood Count, and Disease Activity Monitoring

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann Disease.
evidence.snippet: serum ESR and bone scan as needed in those with acute bone pain to assess disease activity; evaluation of bone mineral density annually in those treated with corticosteroids;

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann Disease.
evidence.snippet: assess blood pressure at each visit; annual CBC.

nodes.description: LAP domain mutation causing premature TGF-beta 1 activation.

Mutations in TGFB1 affect the latency-associated peptide (LAP) region, disrupting the non-covalent association between LAP and mature TGF-beta 1. This leads to premature or excessive release of active TGF-beta 1 from its latent complex, resulting in increased osteoblast activity and cortical bone thickening in the diaphyses.

evidence.reference_title: Domain-specific mutations of a transforming growth factor (TGF)-beta 1 latency-associated peptide cause Camurati-Engelmann disease because of the formation of a constitutively active form of TGF-beta 1.
evidence.snippet: these mutations disrupt the association of beta1-LAP and TGF-beta1 and the subsequent release of the mature TGF-beta1

evidence.reference_title: Domain-specific mutations of a transforming growth factor (TGF)-beta 1 latency-associated peptide cause Camurati-Engelmann disease because of the formation of a constitutively active form of TGF-beta 1.
evidence.snippet: the proliferation of human osteoblastic MG-63 cells was accelerated by coculture with CED fibroblasts

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann Disease.
evidence.snippet: disease-causing mutations are located within the TGFβ-1 gene and expected to or thought to disrupt the binding between TGFβ1 and its latency-associated peptide resulting in an increased signaling of the pathway and subsequently accelerated bone turnover.

nodes.description: Low-dose corticosteroids (deflazacort or prednisone) are the mainstay of treatment. They reduce bone pain and improve muscle weakness, likely through anti-inflammatory effects and suppression of TGF-beta 1 signaling.

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann Disease.
evidence.snippet: Corticosteroid therapy as needed to control symptoms; losartan may be a helpful adjuvant therapy to minimize the need for steroids to control pain.

evidence.reference_title: Domain-specific mutations of a transforming growth factor (TGF)-beta 1 latency-associated peptide cause Camurati-Engelmann disease because of the formation of a constitutively active form of TGF-beta 1.
evidence.snippet: The growth suppression observed was attenuated by neutralizing antibody to TGF-beta1 or by treatment of dexamethasone.

evidence.reference_title: Marked phenotypic variability in progressive diaphyseal dysplasia (Camurati-Engelmann disease): report of a four-generation pedigree, identification of a mutation in TGFB1, and review.
evidence.snippet: Two of the symptomatic individuals were treated successfully with prednisone.

evidence.reference_title: Marked phenotypic variability in progressive diaphyseal dysplasia (Camurati-Engelmann disease): report of a four-generation pedigree, identification of a mutation in TGFB1, and review.
evidence.snippet: Therapy with corticosteroids should be attempted in all symptomatic patients.

nodes.description: Cranial nerve compression due to skull base hyperostosis, occurring in patients with cranial involvement. Can lead to facial paralysis, hearing loss, and increased intracranial pressure.

evidence.reference_title: Marked phenotypic variability in progressive diaphyseal dysplasia (Camurati-Engelmann disease): report of a four-generation pedigree, identification of a mutation in TGFB1, and review.
evidence.snippet: Cranial involvement, which occurs in 61% of patients, can be severe, entrapping cranial nerves or causing increased intracranial pressure.

evidence.reference_title: Marked phenotypic variability in progressive diaphyseal dysplasia (Camurati-Engelmann disease): report of a four-generation pedigree, identification of a mutation in TGFB1, and review.
evidence.snippet: The most severely affected individual had progression of mild skull hyperostosis to severe skull thickening and cranial nerve compression over 30 years.

nodes.description: Progressive cortical thickening (hyperostosis and sclerosis) of the diaphyses of long bones, typically the femora and tibiae. The hallmark radiographic finding, present in 94% of molecularly confirmed cases.

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann disease: review of the clinical, radiological, and molecular data of 24 families and implications for diagnosis and treatment.
evidence.snippet: Radiological symptoms were not fully penetrant, with 94% of the patients showing the typical long bone involvement.

nodes.description: Proptosis from narrowing of the optic foramina due to skull hyperostosis.

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann Disease.
evidence.snippet: Facial features such as macrocephaly, frontal bossing, enlargement of the mandible, proptosis, and cranial nerve impingement resulting in facial palsy are seen in severely affected individuals later in life.

nodes.description: Sensorineural or conductive hearing loss from involvement of the temporal bone and compression of the vestibulocochlear nerve.

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann Disease.
evidence.snippet: Management of hearing loss per otolaryngologist; bilateral myringotomy can improve conductive hearing loss resulting from serous otitis.

nodes.description: Excessive TGF-beta 1 signaling shifts the balance between bone formation and resorption toward increased periosteal and endosteal new bone formation. The resulting cortical thickening narrows the medullary cavity of long bones, contributing to marrow failure and pain.

evidence.reference_title: Domain-specific mutations in TGFB1 result in Camurati-Engelmann disease.
evidence.snippet: Camurati-Engelmann disease (CED, MIM 131300) is an autosomal dominant, progressive diaphyseal dysplasia characterized by hyperosteosis and sclerosis of the diaphyses of long bones.

evidence.reference_title: Significant Improvement of Clinical Symptoms, Bone Lesions, and Bone Turnover after Long-Term Zoledronic Acid Treatment in Patients with a Severe Form of Camurati-Engelmann Disease
evidence.snippet: Cortical thickening of the diaphyses of the long bones with narrowing of the medullary cavity are associated with bone pain, waddling gait, muscular weakness, easy fatigability, and a marfanoid body habitus.

nodes.description: Severe cranial and skull-base hyperostosis can cause increased intracranial pressure, requiring neurologic monitoring and sometimes decompressive surgery.

evidence.reference_title: Marked phenotypic variability in progressive diaphyseal dysplasia (Camurati-Engelmann disease): report of a four-generation pedigree, identification of a mutation in TGFB1, and review.
evidence.snippet: Cranial involvement, which occurs in 61% of patients, can be severe, entrapping cranial nerves or causing increased intracranial pressure.

nodes.description: Chronic limb pain, the most common clinical symptom, present in 68% of patients. Often the presenting complaint in childhood. Pain is typically in the lower extremities and may be debilitating.

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann disease: review of the clinical, radiological, and molecular data of 24 families and implications for diagnosis and treatment.
evidence.snippet: Pain in the extremities was the most common clinical symptom, present in 68% of the patients.

evidence.reference_title: Marked phenotypic variability in progressive diaphyseal dysplasia (Camurati-Engelmann disease): report of a four-generation pedigree, identification of a mutation in TGFB1, and review.
evidence.snippet: Symptomatic relatives presented with lower limb pain and weakness.

nodes.description: Angiotensin receptor blockers have been investigated as TGF-beta pathway modulators. Losartan has shown clinical benefit in eliminating pain and improving exercise capacity and body composition.

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann Disease.
evidence.snippet: losartan may be a helpful adjuvant therapy to minimize the need for steroids to control pain.

evidence.reference_title: Elimination of pain and improvement of exercise capacity in Camurati-Engelmann disease with losartan.
evidence.snippet: losartan treatment led to the complete elimination of the previously severe and incapacitating pain, with an increased ability to walk and perform physical activities.

evidence.reference_title: Elimination of pain and improvement of exercise capacity in Camurati-Engelmann disease with losartan.
evidence.snippet: In light of our findings, losartan may be a useful option in CED management.

nodes.description: Proximal muscle weakness and easy fatigability, present in 39% and 44% of patients respectively. Contributes to reduced exercise tolerance.

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann disease: review of the clinical, radiological, and molecular data of 24 families and implications for diagnosis and treatment.
evidence.snippet: easy fatigability (44%), and muscle weakness (39%) were other important features.

nodes.description: Ophthalmic subspecialty care, low vision services, and neurosurgical decompression when needed address ocular manifestations and raised intracranial pressure from severe cranial sclerosis.

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann Disease.
evidence.snippet: Treatment of ocular manifestations per ophthalmic subspecialist with low vision services as needed; craniectomy may be needed to reduce intracranial pressure

nodes.description: Physical medicine, physical therapy, adaptive devices, and fall precautions address weakness, gait impairment, contractures, and functional mobility.

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann Disease.
evidence.snippet: Management of muscle weakness and gait issues per physical medicine and rehabilitation specialists and physical therapist.

nodes.description: LAP domain mutation disrupting beta1-LAP and TGF-beta1 association.
The most common mutation, located in the LAP domain.

Aberrant TGF-beta 1 signaling activates non-canonical Rho GTPase pathways in osteoclast-lineage cells. This increases osteoclastogenesis, migration, bone resorption, and cytoskeletal remodeling, providing a disease-specific branch from TGFB1 activation to high-turnover cortical sclerosis.

evidence.reference_title: Aberrant activation of TGF-β1 induces high bone turnover via Rho GTPases-mediated cytoskeletal remodeling in Camurati-Engelmann disease
evidence.snippet: activation of Rho by TGF-β1 increased osteoclast formation and bone resorption, with increased migration of pre-osteoclasts, as well as cytoskeletal remodeling of pre-osteoclasts and mature osteoclasts.

evidence.reference_title: Aberrant activation of TGF-β1 induces high bone turnover via Rho GTPases-mediated cytoskeletal remodeling in Camurati-Engelmann disease
evidence.snippet: pharmacological inhibition of Rho GTPases effectively rescued hyperactive TGF-β1-induced osteoclastogenesis in vitro.

nodes.description: Hyperostosis and sclerosis of the skull, occurring in 54% of patients. May lead to cranial nerve compression, increased intracranial pressure, and cosmetic changes.

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann disease: review of the clinical, radiological, and molecular data of 24 families and implications for diagnosis and treatment.
evidence.snippet: A large percentage of the patients also showed involvement of the skull (54%) and pelvis (63%).

nodes.description: Endosteal cortical thickening can narrow the medullary cavity of long bones, linking the radiographic bone phenotype to pain, gait impairment, weakness, and possible hematologic complications.

evidence.reference_title: Significant Improvement of Clinical Symptoms, Bone Lesions, and Bone Turnover after Long-Term Zoledronic Acid Treatment in Patients with a Severe Form of Camurati-Engelmann Disease
evidence.snippet: Cortical thickening of the diaphyses of the long bones with narrowing of the medullary cavity are associated with bone pain, waddling gait, muscular weakness, easy fatigability, and a marfanoid body habitus.

evidence.reference_title: Domain-specific mutations in TGFB1 result in Camurati-Engelmann disease.
evidence.snippet: As the human transforming growth factor-1 gene (TGFB1) is located within this interval, we considered it a candidate gene for CED.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the gene encoding the latency-associated peptide of TGF-beta 1 cause Camurati-Engelmann disease.
evidence.snippet: this region contains the gene encoding transforming growth factor-beta 1 (TGFB1), an important mediator of bone remodelling, we evaluated TGFB1 as a candidate gene for causing CED.

evidence.reference_title: Domain-specific mutations of a transforming growth factor (TGF)-beta 1 latency-associated peptide cause Camurati-Engelmann disease because of the formation of a constitutively active form of TGF-beta 1.
evidence.snippet: three different missense mutations (R218H, R218C, and C225R) of beta1-LAP cause the Camurati-Engelmann disease (CED)

evidence.reference_title: Marked phenotypic variability in progressive diaphyseal dysplasia (Camurati-Engelmann disease): report of a four-generation pedigree, identification of a mutation in TGFB1, and review.
evidence.snippet: We screened the TGFB1 gene for mutations and identified a missense mutation resulting in an R218H substitution in the affected individuals

nodes.description: Waddling gait due to proximal muscle weakness and lower limb involvement, present in 48% of patients.

evidence.reference_title: Camurati-Engelmann disease: review of the clinical, radiological, and molecular data of 24 families and implications for diagnosis and treatment.
evidence.snippet: A waddling gait (48%), easy fatigability (44%), and muscle weakness (39%) were other important features.

nodes.description: Bisphosphonate therapy with zoledronic acid is not established first-line CED care, but severe case reports describe improvement in pain, ambulation, bone lesions, body habitus, and bone-turnover markers.

evidence.reference_title: Significant Improvement of Clinical Symptoms, Bone Lesions, and Bone Turnover after Long-Term Zoledronic Acid Treatment in Patients with a Severe Form of Camurati-Engelmann Disease
evidence.snippet: In both probands, zoledronic acid treatment significantly improved the clinical symptoms, bone lesions, ambulation, and body habitus.

evidence.reference_title: Significant Improvement of Clinical Symptoms, Bone Lesions, and Bone Turnover after Long-Term Zoledronic Acid Treatment in Patients with a Severe Form of Camurati-Engelmann Disease
evidence.snippet: Zoledronic acid treatment may be an important therapeutic option in patients with severe CED.
